ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Beyti Kebab. FOODON_00002764 (kebab food product) is the closest defensible parent class. Tandoori chicken variant is not separately coded.
ontology.foodex2_description: Kebab — mixed meat and bread composite dish
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers kebab-type composite dishes. No specific Beyti Kebab or tandoori chicken wrap code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Beyti Kebab or tandoori chicken wrap dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of Beyti Kebab with Tandoori Chicken weighs approximately 400 g and includes grilled tandoori chicken wrapped in lavash bread, topped with tomato sauce and yogurt.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat only, cooked, roasted (FDC ID 171477) and Bread, pita, white, enriched (FDC ID 174924), combined with ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017) for tandoori spice profile
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN), India
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Beyti Kebab with Tandoori Chicken as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for roasted chicken (FDC ID 171477), white flatbread/pita (FDC ID 174924), plain yogurt (FDC ID 170886), and tomato sauce (FDC ID 172945), weighted to reflect the dish composition (~50% chicken, ~25% lavash, ~15% yogurt/sauce, ~10% vegetables/spices). Input record values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are consistent with this component-weighted estimate and have been retained with minor rounding. ICMR-NIN consulted for tandoori spice nutrient contribution context.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database — Flatbread / pita analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Beyti Kebab with Tandoori Chicken. GI estimated at 52 (low) based on the composite dish profile: lavash/pita flatbread GI ~57 (University of Sydney GI Database, white pita), moderated downward by high protein (13.75 g/100g) and fat (10 g/100g) content which slow gastric emptying and blunt postprandial glucose response. Composite GI estimated using macronutrient-weighted blending approach consistent with Brand-Miller methodology. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 45.0) / 100 = 23.4 (carbohydrate per 400 g serving = 11.25 × 4 = 45.0 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg/100 g; estimated 1200 mg per standard 400 g serving
hypertension.guidance: A 400 g serving delivers approximately 1200 mg sodium (52% of the 2300 mg daily limit), so individuals with hypertension should request reduced salt in the marinade and tomato sauce, limit portion size to 200–250 g, and balance the meal with potassium-rich vegetables; estimated potassium (~280 mg/100 g) provides partial offset but does not fully neutralise the sodium load at full-plate portions.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate (11.25 g/100 g) from refined lavash bread; estimated composite GI ~52 (low range)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The dish has a low estimated GI (~52) and moderate carbohydrate content (11.25 g/100 g), with high protein (13.75 g/100 g) and fat (10 g/100 g) slowing gastric emptying; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit the lavash bread component, request wholemeal lavash where available, and keep the serving to 200–250 g to maintain postprandial glucose within target range.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 3.0 g/100 g and sodium 300 mg/100 g; cholesterol 45 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Saturated fat is relatively controlled at 3.0 g/100 g and the dish provides anti-inflammatory benefit from turmeric and olive oil (MUFA), but the sodium content (300 mg/100 g) and cholesterol (45 mg/100 g) warrant portion control to ≤200 g for individuals with established cardiovascular disease or dyslipidaemia, and preparation should use minimal added oil and reduced-salt tomato sauce.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory spices (turmeric, garlic) and MUFA from olive oil; nightshade ingredients present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Turmeric (curcumin) and garlic in the marinade provide anti-inflammatory polyphenols, and olive oil contributes oleocanthal (anti-inflammatory MUFA); however, tomato sauce and bell peppers are nightshade ingredients containing solanine, and individuals with nightshade sensitivity should monitor for symptom flares and consider requesting a nightshade-free sauce variant.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beyti Kebab with Tandoori Chicken (贝提烤鸡串; Kebab Beyti Ayam Tandoori) is a Turkish-origin composite dish adapted in Singapore's multicultural food scene, combining the flavours of Turkish kebab tradition with South Asian tandoori cooking. The dish consists of tandoori-marinated chicken — seasoned with turmeric, cumin, paprika, garlic, and yogurt — grilled or roasted in a tandoor or conventional oven until charred and juicy, then wrapped tightly in lavash flatbread. The roll is sliced into rounds and served on a plate topped with a rich tomato-based sauce and a generous dollop of plain yogurt. The result is a layered flavour profile: smoky and spiced chicken, soft and slightly chewy lavash, tangy tomato sauce, and cooling yogurt. Nutritionally, the dish provides 187.5 kcal per 100 g with a strong protein contribution of 13.75 g/100 g from the chicken, moderate fat at 10 g/100 g (predominantly MUFA from olive oil), and moderate carbohydrate at 11.25 g/100 g from the lavash bread. Sodium is 300 mg/100 g, making portion control relevant for hypertension management. The estimated composite glycemic index is approximately 52 (low), moderated by the high protein and fat content. The dish is halal-certified in most Singapore restaurant contexts and is suitable for general consumption with portion awareness for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Beyti Kebab with Tandoori Chicken as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for roasted chicken, white flatbread, plain yogurt, and tomato sauce, weighted by approximate dish composition. Input record values (ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are consistent with the component-weighted estimate. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database flatbread data, moderated by macronutrient profile. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data (roasted chicken FDC 171477, white pita FDC 174924, plain yogurt FDC 170886, tomato sauce FDC 172945) weighted ~50% chicken, ~25% lavash, ~15% yogurt/sauce, ~10% vegetables/spices. Input ai_generated values retained as consistent with this estimate. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient breakdown for this composite dish preparation is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database; component-level data exists but weighted composite values for these micronutrients were not computed to avoid compounding estimation error. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (3.8 g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.9 g) estimated from USDA FoodData Central roasted chicken and olive oil component data; potassium_mg (280 mg) estimated from chicken and tomato sauce components. 4. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Beyti Kebab exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 5. FoodOn URI FOODON_00002764 and FoodEx2 code A0FJP are approximate matches; no exact ontology code for Beyti Kebab exists as of 2024. 6. Although this dish is Turkish in culinary origin, the input record designates country_of_origin as Singapore and region as Southeast Asia; accordingly, G6 multilingual naming compliance has been applied for Singaporean dishes, and local_names and aliases have been populated in Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi. Chinese simplified: 贝提烤鸡串; Chinese traditional: 貝提烤雞串; Malay: Kebab Beyti Ayam Tandoori; Tamil: பெய்தி கபாப் தந்தூரி சிக்கன்; Hindi: बेयती कबाब तंदूरी चिकन.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft created from input JSON with USDA FoodData Central component estimation.
Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent'. Attempted to argue Turkish cuisine exemption from G6 Singaporean multilingual rule.

Iteration 3: Applied G6 Singaporean multilingual naming compliance based on country_of_origin = Singapore in input record. Populated local_names for Chinese Simplified (贝提烤鸡串), Chinese Traditional (貝提烤雞串), Malay (Kebab Beyti Ayam Tandoori), Tamil (பெய்தி கபாப் தந்தூரி சிக்கன்), and Hindi (बेयती कबाब तंदूरी चिकन). Added all five local-language names to flat aliases array and aliases_by_language. Q1 and Q8 now pass. G6 hard gate now passes.
